title: onobrychis crista-galli
intro: Onobrychis crista-galli, also known as Squirrel Tail or Crested Locoweed, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its distinctive hairy stems and pink or purple flowers. The medicinal properties of Onobrychis crista-galli include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce pain, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, as well as its use in treating menstrual disorders and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Onobrychis crista-galli include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the flowers, leaves, or roots in hot water or a solvent, and can be used internally or topically.
